_readme: EDITING GUIDE — (1) To activate MagicEden buttons, replace every 'PLACEHOLDER' magicEdenUrl with the real URL (per line, and collectionMagicEdenUrl for the global button). Until then the buttons render as a disabled 'soon' state. (2) To add a future mint, append NFT objects with the correct 'line' and a new 'mint' number, then run scripts/build-images.py to generate their webp images. (3) Titles/descriptions are public-facing copy; 'alt' is accessibility text describing the actual image.

collection.name: 401jK NFT Collection
collection.intro: 401jK explores the cracks in the system we were told would take care of us. Through satire, history, rebellion, and defiance, each series captures a different way people wake up, opt out, and retire on their own terms. As the collection grows, so does the story, because freedom isn't one path, one look, or one finish line.

lines.name: Legends Rewritten
lines.aesthetic: Pop art · bold colour · satirical portraits
lines.description: A pop-art rebellion remixing history's icons with 401jK's satirical punchline. Each portrait transforms a cultural legend into a co-conspirator exposing the absurdity of the retirement system. Bright, bold, and brutally honest, because even the greats knew the rigged system is a joke played on us.

lines.name: Rebellion Reframed
lines.aesthetic: Classical art · dramatic historical scenes recontextualised
lines.description: A dramatic fusion of classical art and modern financial revolt. Reframes humanity's defining uprisings through 401jK's lens. Each piece exposes how little has changed: elites still write the rules, and the rest of us are expected to applaud. This is rebellion retold.

lines.name: The Jester's Tarot
lines.description: A series that reimagines tarot as a 401jK journey from chaos to clarity. Through defiant Jester cards, explores nonconformity, corruption, collapse, transformation, and the choice to take back your own path. Symbolic, satirical, and surprisingly on point.

nfts.title: Winston Churchill
nfts.alt: A neon, glitch-art poster of Winston Churchill holding a Tommy gun, featuring a quote about jokes.
nfts.description: Winston Churchill (1874-1965) was the British prime minister who held the line against fascism when surrender looked like the sensible option. "A joke is a very serious thing," and so is the 401jK. We meme because the people who rigged the system want you to think the fight is unserious. It isn't.

nfts.title: Thomas Paine
nfts.alt: A glitch-art portrait of Thomas Paine wearing futuristic sunglasses, with a quote about rebellion.
nfts.description: Thomas Paine (1737-1809) was the writer whose words lit the fuse on the American Revolution and made the case that common people could govern themselves. He believed rebellion becomes a duty once the system stops serving you. The 401jK picks up where he left off: the rigged financial order took everything, so rebellion is the only move.

nfts.title: Nikola Tesla
nfts.alt: A distorted, glitch-art image of Nikola Tesla with his magnifying transmitter, featuring a quote about electricity and hate.
nfts.description: Nikola Tesla (1856-1943) was the Serbian-American inventor who gave the world alternating current (the electrical system still powering civilization) and died nearly broke after being outmaneuvered by the men who profited from his genius. "If your hate could be turned into electricity, it would light up the whole world." The 401jK runs on exactly that energy: channeling the frustration at a rigged system into something that can't be switched off.

nfts.title: Ralph Waldo Emerson
nfts.alt: A neon, distorted portrait of Ralph Waldo Emerson wearing futuristic sunglasses, with a quote about cause and effect.
nfts.description: Ralph Waldo Emerson (1803-1882) was the American philosopher and poet who made self-reliance a doctrine and told a generation that their own mind was sufficient authority. "Shallow men believe in luck. Strong men believe in cause and effect." 401jK is cause and effect: a movement built on the simple logic that when you stop trusting the system's rules, you stop being bound by them.

nfts.title: Chief Sitting Bull
nfts.alt: A vibrant portrait of Chief Sitting Bull wearing a headdress and modern sunglasses, featuring a quote about unity.
nfts.description: Chief Sitting Bull (c.1831-1890) was the Hunkpapa Lakota war chief who led his people's resistance against a government that made promises it had no intention of keeping. "As individual fingers we can easily be broken, but all together we make a mighty fist." The 401jK community is the fist. The system counted on us staying fingers.

nfts.title: Chief Joseph
nfts.alt: A red glitch-art portrait of Chief Joseph wearing modern sunglasses, featuring a quote about big names.
nfts.description: Chief Joseph (1840-1904) was the Nez Perce chief who led his people on a 1,400-mile retreat toward freedom, outmaneuvering the US Army at every turn before surrendering with the words: "I will fight no more forever." "Big name often stands on small legs." The 401jK knows the institutions built to impress us are mostly hollow. Their authority is borrowed.

nfts.title: Harriet Tubman
nfts.alt: A gold-toned glitch-art portrait of Harriet Tubman wearing modern sunglasses, with a quote about snakes.
nfts.description: Harriet Tubman (c.1822-1913) was the woman who escaped enslavement and came back thirteen times to lead others to freedom when she could have simply disappeared into safety. "Never wound a snake; kill it." 401jK doesn't poke the retirement system and hope it changes. It builds the exit route. Harriet would understand.

nfts.title: Marie Curie
nfts.alt: A blue glitch-art silhouette of Marie Curie in a laboratory setting, featuring a quote about progress.
nfts.description: Marie Curie (1867-1934) was the physicist who won the Nobel Prize twice (in two different sciences) in an era that didn't think women belonged in either one. She rewrote the laws of matter while the system pretended she wasn't in the room. "I was taught that the way of progress is neither swift nor easy." 401jK was never a shortcut. It's the long game, played by people who refuse to wait for permission.

nfts.title: Emmeline Pankhurst
nfts.alt: A purple glitch-art historical photo of Emmeline Pankhurst addressing a crowd, featuring a quote about broken windows.
nfts.description: Emmeline Pankhurst (1858-1928) was the British activist who led the suffragette movement after decades of polite protest got women nowhere. She decided disruption was the only language the system understood. "The argument of the broken window pane is the most valuable argument in modern politics." The 401jK runs on the same idea. Asking nicely never fixed a rigged system. Disruption does.

nfts.title: Henry David Thoreau
nfts.alt: A green-tinted portrait of Henry David Thoreau wearing modern meme-style sunglasses against a patterned background.
nfts.description: Henry David Thoreau (1817-1862) was the American philosopher and author who walked into the woods to prove a point: that a life deliberately chosen on your own terms is worth more than any salary or title the system could offer. "I have great faith in a seed - convince me you have one, and I am prepared to expect wonders." 401jK is a seed. Small, stubborn, and irreverent. The wonders are already in motion.

nfts.title: Liberty Leading the People
nfts.alt: A satirical edit of the painting 'Liberty Leading the People', featuring the central figure wearing meme sunglasses and holding a '401JK' flag.
nfts.description: Reimagining of 'Liberty Leading the People' by Eugène Delacroix, 1830. The painting shows Liberty leading the people during the July Revolution, and now she leads the 401jK community. Same fight. New enemy: a system that makes you wait forty years for permission to retire.

nfts.title: Surrender of Lord Cornwallis
nfts.alt: An edited classical painting of military figures where the central leader wears meme sunglasses and two flags display '401JK'.
nfts.description: Reimagining of 'Surrender of Lord Cornwallis' by John Trumbull, 1820. The painting shows the mighty British empire laying down its arms to a ragtag colonial army it swore could never win. Now the 401jK community is the underdog, facing down the rigged financial system. And just as then, the underdog will win against all odds.

nfts.title: The Spirit of '76
nfts.alt: A modified version of the painting 'The Spirit of '76', with the flag bearer holding a '401JK' flag.
nfts.description: Reimagining of 'The Spirit of '76' by Archibald Willard, 1876. The painting shows three battered musicians marching on through smoke, refusing to stop the beat, and now they drum for the 401jK movement. We keep marching. We keep memeing. The beat doesn't stop.

nfts.title: Washington at Trenton
nfts.alt: An edited classical portrait of George Washington wearing meme sunglasses and a large gold chain with a '401jK' pendant.
nfts.description: Reimagining of 'General George Washington at Trenton' by John Trumbull, 1792. The original shows Washington calm before a battle everyone said he'd lose, on the night that turned a dying cause around. Now he stands for the 401jK movement. Same long odds. New impossible crossing: opting out of a rigged retirement.

nfts.title: The Storming of the Bastille
nfts.alt: An altered painting of the Storming of the Bastille, featuring revolutionaries wearing meme sunglasses and a '401JK' flag.
nfts.description: Reimagining of 'The Storming of the Bastille' by Jean-Pierre Houël, 1789. The original shows an armed Paris mob tearing down a symbol of royal tyranny on July 14, 1789, the spark that lit the French Revolution. Now the 401jK flag flies over the breach. The fortress changed. The fight didn't.

nfts.title: Reply of the Zaporozhian Cossacks
nfts.alt: A humorous edit of the painting 'Reply of the Zaporozhian Cossacks', where multiple figures wear meme sunglasses and a '401JK' flag flies in the background.
nfts.description: Reimagining of 'Reply of the Zaporozhian Cossacks' by Ilya Repin, 1891. The original shows a crowd of Cossacks roaring with laughter as they compose the most gloriously defiant and obscene letter in history, a reply to a Sultan who demanded their surrender. The 401jK community writes that letter to the financial system now, one meme at a time. Different empire. Same defiance.

nfts.title: Kościuszko at Racławice
nfts.alt: A modified historical battle painting featuring soldiers in meme sunglasses, a '401JK' flag, and a banner reading 'OCCUPY RETIREMENT'.
nfts.description: Reimagining of 'Kościuszko at the Battle of Racławice' by Jan Matejko, 1888. The painting shows Polish peasant farmers, armed with nothing but scythes, crushing a professional army against every sensible prediction. Their general bet everything on the people the system had written off. The 401jK community are the farmers rebelling against the system. And memes are our scythes.

nfts.title: Storming the Winter Palace
nfts.alt: An edited painting of a colonial-era battle where key commanders wear meme sunglasses and a flag reads '401JK'.
nfts.description: Reimagining of 'Storming the Winter Palace' by Vladimir Serov, 1938. The painting shows the night the Bolsheviks took history's wheel and ended an order everyone swore would last forever. The 401jK community storms a different palace now: the rigged retirement system that calls itself permanent. Systems built on extraction and inequality will collapse. This one just hasn't been told yet.

nfts.title: Oath of the Thirty-Three
nfts.alt: An edited painting of soldiers celebrating around a '401JK' flag, with several figures wearing meme sunglasses.
nfts.description: Reimagining of 'El Juramento de los Treinta y Tres Orientales' by Juan Manuel Blanes, 1877. The painting shows thirty-three Uruguayan patriots raising their swords to swear they would free their homeland against all odds. The 401jK community is the thirty-three: small, outnumbered, and sworn to the fight anyway. Not with swords. With conviction. Same fire.

nfts.title: Blessing the Flag of Revolution
nfts.alt: A modified painting of an assembly in an ornate church, where a bishop and others wear meme sunglasses and hold a '401JK' flag.
nfts.description: Reimagining of 'The Bishop of Old Patras Germanos Blesses the Flag of Revolution' by Theodoros Vryzakis, 1865. The original shows a bishop lifting the flag of revolution and an entire people choosing, in that instant, to stop complying and start fighting for their freedom. The 401jK community makes the same choice. Different century. Same refusal to live on someone else's terms.

nfts.title: The Fool
nfts.alt: A vibrant tarot card titled 'THE FOOL' featuring a jester wearing futuristic goggles surfing on a cosmic wave.
nfts.description: The ultimate nonconformist. The Jester steps off the edge into the unknown, unbothered by societal expectations or rules. The Fool represents marching to the beat of your own drum and rejecting traditional systems in favor of the Jester's own path.

nfts.title: The Magician
nfts.alt: A golden-toned tarot card titled 'THE MAGICIAN' showing a goggled jester holding a glowing wand over a table with mystical objects.
nfts.description: This Jester represents the power to manipulate or change your environment. When standing against a system, the Magician shows that you have the individual agency, tools, and willpower to create your own reality outside of established limitations.

nfts.title: The High Priestess
nfts.alt: A dark, mystical tarot card titled 'THE HIGH PRIESTESS' featuring a seated woman with a jester hat, goggles, and an all-seeing eye book.
nfts.description: This Jester represents intuition, inner knowing, and the quiet awareness that exists beneath the surface of what is seen. She reflects a state of understanding that doesn't need to be explained or proven, where truth is felt rather than spoken. She appears when you are being asked to trust your instincts, pay attention to subtle signals, and listen to what you already sense deep down. It is not about action, but about stillness and perception, where clarity comes from within rather than from external validation.

nfts.title: The Hierophant (Reversed)
nfts.alt: A dark tarot card titled 'THE HIEROPHANT' showing a papal figure with a jester hat hanging upside down.
nfts.description: The upright Hierophant represents organized religion, traditional institutions, and societal rules. When reversed, it signifies a direct rebellion from the Jester against these dogmatic structures, rejecting conventional wisdom, or choosing an unconventional lifestyle.

nfts.title: The Moon
nfts.alt: A surreal tarot card titled 'THE MOON' depicting two kneeling jesters vomiting glowing green liquid beneath a massive, crying moon wearing sunglasses.
nfts.description: The Moon represents confusion, illusion, and not being able to trust what you're seeing or understanding. This Jester shows up when things are unclear, emotions are heightened, and when fear, uncertainty, or doubt starts filling in the gaps to replace the truth. This is a space of intuition and subconscious influence, but also of distortion, where perception can easily be shaped by anxiety, projection, or hidden information.

nfts.title: The Jeet
nfts.alt: A dark tarot card titled 'THE JEET' showing a grotesque goblin hoarding gold coins in a flooded, gloomy urban underpass.
nfts.description: The Jeet, or the Just Exit Early Trader, represents fear-driven scarcity, panic, and the inability to see beyond the next small gain. This gremlin appears when you adamantly proclaim your Jester loyalty, but abandon conviction for comfort, clutching at scraps while greater opportunities decay around them. The Jeet lives beneath the ruins of what could have been, obsessed with collecting coins one by one while remaining blind to the larger path forward. It reflects the mindset of survival over vision, where impatience, fear, uncertainty, doubt, and emotional reactions keep a person trapped in cycles of loss and desperation.

nfts.title: Death
nfts.alt: A dark tarot card titled 'DEATH' depicting a reaper in a jester hat and sunglasses holding a scythe and an hourglass.
nfts.description: This Jester represents a necessary ending that clears away what is no longer meant to continue, making space for something new to emerge. It signals a moment of irreversible change where an old version of your life, identity, or belief, is coming to a close. While uncomfortable, it is not about loss for the sake of loss, but about transformation and release. This Jester asks you to accept what is already ending, because holding on will only hinder true growth.

nfts.title: The Devil
nfts.alt: A fiery tarot card titled 'THE DEVIL' featuring a winged jester figure on a throne, surrounded by chained, bowing jesters.
nfts.description: This Jester represents being trapped in patterns, attachments, or desires that are powerful, but ultimately enslave you. He shows up when you begin to acknowledge that how you cope, manage, or seek release is self-destructive, but you continue to engage with it regardless. This Jester highlights the cycles of dependency, avoidance, and self-sabotage, and asks you to recognize where you are giving away that power.

nfts.title: The Tower
nfts.alt: A cosmic tarot card titled 'THE TOWER' showing a stone tower struck by a red force, with a floating jester hat and goggles above.
nfts.description: The 401jK Tower (CDIJK) represents sudden, disruptive, and often unavoidable change, destruction, and chaos, signaling a necessary collapse of unstable structures, beliefs, or situations. Often associated with sudden enlightenment, the Jester strips away illusions, forcing a painful but liberating "back-to-the-basics" restart.

nfts.title: Justice (Reversed)
nfts.alt: A tarot card titled 'JUSTICE' depicting an upside-down jester holding a sword and a set of scales.
nfts.description: This Jester questions the fairness of established laws, systems, or institutions. Reversed, it can indicate corruption, unfair policies, or a need to fight back against a flawed system that does not serve the Jester's best interests.

nfts.title: Judgement
nfts.alt: A vibrant tarot card titled 'JUDGEMENT' featuring a winged jester blowing a golden trumpet from the heavens.
nfts.description: This Jester represents a moment of awakening, where clarity breaks through and you are called to see yourself and your life honestly. It reflects a turning point where past actions, patterns, or choices come into full awareness, and you are being asked to rise into a new version of yourself. This is not about punishment, but about realization and accountability, where you can no longer ignore what you now understand. This Jester signals a second chance, a rebirth, or a chance to move forward with a deeper sense of purpose and truth.

nfts.title: Two of Fucks
nfts.alt: A psychedelic tarot card titled 'TWO OF FUCKS' showing a seated, blindfolded jester holding up both middle fingers.
nfts.description: This Jester represents radical self-ownership, unapologetic individuality, and the freedom that comes from no longer living for the approval of others. He appears when you are being called to stop shrinking yourself to fit expectations, opinions, or social pressure, and instead move through life on your own terms with confidence and authenticity. The Jester in this card understands that not everyone will approve of his choices, path, or identity, and he no longer wastes energy trying to make them comfortable with who he is. While the card carries rebellious energy, it is not about cruelty or arrogance, but about releasing the exhausting need for validation and embracing the adventure of being fully yourself.
